description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
On May 14, 2025 at 6:54 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Los Angeles, California was in a collision 
involving a passenger car on Venice Boulevard near Frances Avenue. 
 
The Waymo AV was in autonomous mode traveling northeast on Venice Boulevard in the right lane. As the Waymo AV reached the 
intersection with Frances Avenue, a dog entered the roadway from the southeast sidewalk. The Waymo AV began braking and then the test 
driver assumed manual control of the Waymo AV and continued braking. As the Waymo AV came to a stop, the dog continued to cross in 
front of the Waymo AV. A passenger car traveling behind the Waymo AV swerved to the left, and the front passenger side of the passenger 
car made contact with the rear driver side of the Waymo AV. Following the initial contact, the passenger car continued into the lane to the 
left of the Waymo AV, struck the dog, and then came to a stop in the left lane. The dog then left the scene. At the time of the impact, the 
Waymo AV’s Level 4 ADS was not engaged and a test driver was operating the Waymo AV in manual mode. Both vehicles sustained 
damage.
Print
Clear Form
